title: Netflix's Most Anticipated Show of 2026 Drops — And It's Already Breaking Records
excerpt: Netflix's star-studded new limited series 'Infamous' premiered to record-breaking viewership numbers, with 45 million households tuning in within the first 72 hours — making it the platform's biggest debut of the year.
content:
Netflix has done it again. The streaming giant's newest prestige drama Infamous, a true-crime limited series following the rise and fall of a fictional Hollywood power couple, has shattered the platform's viewership records, pulling in a staggering 45 million households within its first 72 hours — the biggest debut for any Netflix original in 2026.

The show, which stars Ana de Armas and Idris Elba as the charismatic but morally complex couple at the center of a glittering Hollywood scandal, has been praised by critics as "compulsively watchable" and "the prestige TV event of the year."

What Is Infamous About?

Infamous follows Victoria and Marcus Hartwell, a beloved Hollywood couple who seem to have everything — fame, fortune, and the admiration of millions. But when a whistleblower releases damaging documents days before a major awards show, their carefully constructed world begins to unravel in shocking and unexpected ways.

The show draws clear inspiration from real-life Hollywood scandals but is entirely fictional, according to showrunner Ryan Murphy, who co-created the series with Issa Rae. "We wanted to make something that felt real, felt raw, and made audiences question everything they think they know about fame," Murphy told Variety.

Star-Studded Supporting Cast

Beyond de Armas and Elba, the show features an ensemble cast including Zendaya as a ruthless entertainment journalist, Timothée Chalamet as the Hartwell's son entangled in the scandal, and Viola Davis as the couple's formidable attorney.

Critical Reaction

Infamous currently holds a 96% rating on Rotten Tomatoes, with critics calling it "the most daring, stylish, and emotionally devastating show Netflix has ever produced." The Hollywood Reporter called Ana de Armas's performance "the performance of her career and the frontrunner for every major awards circuit in 2027."

When Can You Watch?

All 8 episodes of Infamous are now streaming exclusively on Netflix. A second season has already been greenlit following the record-breaking premiere numbers.

metaDescription: Netflix's 'Infamous' breaks records with 45M households in 72 hours — starring Ana de Armas and Idris Elba in the streaming hit of 2026.
